metadata.title: Accelerated Decline in Hepatic Glucose Production During Fasting in Normal Women Compared With Men
metadata.study_design: Comparative metabolic study with sequential 3-3H-glucose infusions after 14 and 64-hour fasts
metadata.demographics: Five normal men and five normal women in the midfollicular phase of menstrual cycle, matched for age and body mass index, with no history of diabetes mellitus, hypertension, kidney disease, or medication use

evidence.quote: Accelerated Decline in Hepatic Glucose Production During Fasting in Normal Women Compared With Men
John N. Clore, Penny S. Glickman, Shirley T. Helm, John E. Nestler, and William G. Blackard
five normal men and five normal women underwent sequentiel S3H-glucose infusions after both a 14 and a 64hour fast

Five of the subjects were male and five were female in the midfollicular phase of their menstrual cycle. None had a history of diabetes mellitus, hypertension, or kidney disease or were on any medications. Subjects were matched for age and body mass index (BMI).
five normal men and five normal women underwent sequentiel S3H-glucose infusions

methods_participants.label: Inclusion criteria - health status
methods_participants.value: None had a history of diabetes mellitus, hypertension, or kidney disease or were on any medications

evidence.quote: None had a history of diabetes mellitus, hypertension, or kidney disease or were on any medications

methods_participants.label: Assay - glucose measurement
methods_participants.method: Gammacoat assay, Clinical Assays, Cambridge, MA

evidence.quote: Plasma glucose was measured immediately after centrifugation by the glucose oxidase method (Beckman, Fullerton, CA)
Plasma insulin, C-peptide, growth hormone, and glucagon were determined using double-antibody radioimmunoassays
A primed (25 &i) continuous (0.25 &i) infusion of 3-3H-glucose (New England Nuclear, Boston) was administered via a forearm vein from 7 AM until 10 AM

methods_participants.label: Blood sampling timing
methods_participants.value: 20-minute intervals during the last hour of the study (9 AM to 10 AM)

evidence.quote: blood samples were obtained at 20-minute intervals during the last hour of the study (9 AM to 10 AM)

methods_participants.label: Body composition assessment method 1
methods_participants.method: BIA-101 Body Composition Analyzer, RJL Systems, Detroit

evidence.quote: Body composition was assessed by skinfold measurements and impedance plethysmography (BIA-101 Body Composition Analyzer, RJL Systems, Detroit)

narrative_results.statement: Plasma levels of glucose, lactate, and FFA fell significantly in women when put to bed rest after a 64-hour fast

evidence.quote: When put to bed rest, plasma levels of these metabolites, as well as of glucose, fell significantly in women.

narrative_results.statement: Changes in plasma glucose were associated with decreases in plasma insulin and C-peptide as well as increases in plasma glucagon

evidence.quote: Changes in plasma glucose were associated with decreases in plasma insulin and C-peptide as well as increases in plasma glucagon (Table 2).

narrative_results.statement: All subjects exhibited at least a sixfold increase in β-hydroxybutyrate levels during the fast

evidence.quote: all subjects exhibited at least a sixfold increase during the fast.

narrative_results.statement: No change in plasma glucose, lactate, and FFA was observed in men during bed rest after a 64-hour fast

evidence.quote: In contrast, no change was observed in men.
